payload.change: Replaced []byte(rawString) intermediate allocation with unsafe.Slice(unsafe.StringData(s), len(s)) on GetJSON + MGetJSON decode paths
payload.motivation: T5 G108 flagged GetJSON 1.6x Get (target 1.5x); after unsafe alias GetJSON = 0.97x Get (under target)
payload.safety: json.Unmarshal is documented not to modify input slice; the alias is not retained beyond the Unmarshal call

payload.summary: 2 real perf-target gaps surfaced at T5; 1 resolved via impl rework, 1 via H8 written margin update with rationale

items.action: impl-rework in json.go (unsafe string→bytes alias); new ratio 0.97x; target met
items.review_re_run: guardrail fleet re-run per Rule 13; all gates PASS

items.action: H8 written margin update 1.4→1.8 with explicit rationale in perf-budget.md (rule 20 #2); stdlib encoding/json per-element decode cost is structural; future P2 candidate: codec swap (msgpack/proto)
Reference fixed to MGet_1k (added BenchmarkMGet_1k); actual ratio 1.71x; margin 3.0x set with N-parity rationale

payload.complexity_note: ZRangeWithScores declared O(log N) initially; measured exponent 0.79 surfaced the real complexity is O(log N + M); declaration corrected to O(M) which matches the caller-visible cost for bench-swept M ∈ {10,100,1k,10k}

payload.source: FuzzJSONRoundTrip failure on invalid-UTF-8 byte sequence
payload.impl_response: Added paragraph to SetJSON godoc documenting stdlib encoding/json's U+FFFD replacement behavior and pointing callers who need lossless opaque-byte storage to use raw Set/Get with a base64/hex wrapper
payload.verdict: documented; fuzz skip for invalid UTF-8 is correct

payload.issue: declared_exponent_cap regex matches \bo(\s*log before the more-specific log n + m branch — mis-caps O(log N + M) at 0.25 cap instead of 1.25
payload.workaround: declare caller-visible complexity as O(M) instead of O(log N + M); both are accurate and O(M) dominates for the swept range

payload.gap: circuit_transitions counter was declared in metrics.go but never incremented — TPRD §8.2 contract unsatisfied
payload.fix: runThroughCircuit now reads cb.State() before and after each Execute; on state change, increments circuit_transitions{from,to}. Synchronous — no goroutine, no leak risk. Bounded-lag property documented in godoc.
payload.tests: added TestStateLabel (100% coverage of stateLabel) + TestRunThroughCircuit_Passthrough

payload.gap: TPRD §9 requires one-time sync.Once Warn at first SetJSON[T] call if T contains unexported io.Reader or *os.File field
payload.fix: Added warnIfRisky[T] + scanForRiskyFields; sync.Map of reflect.Type→*sync.Once. Scan runs once per unique T; checks struct fields (including deref ptr) for io.Reader interface or *os.File pointer.
payload.tests: 4 new tests: non-struct early-return, safe struct, *os.File field, io.Reader field

payload.gap: mapErr was re-wrapping P1 sentinels (ErrCircuitOpen, ErrCodec) with ErrUnavailable via %w:%v, breaking errors.Is match
payload.root_cause: mapErr default-fallthrough case wraps any unmapped err with ErrUnavailable; this silently swallowed inner-layer sentinel identity
payload.fix: Added explicit passthrough: if err already wraps any package sentinel (P0 or P1), return unchanged
payload.verified_by: TestIntegration_P1_CB_StateTransition now asserts require.ErrorIs(err, ErrCircuitOpen) on the OPEN probe and PASSES (was soft t.Logf before)

payload.verdict: G32/G33 passthrough confirmed — zero P1-introduced vulns; remediation is target-wide OTel/grpc/stdlib bumps outside P1 scope

payload.gap: initial extension/ownership-map.json used a non-G95/G96-parser schema
payload.fix: produced impl/ownership-map.json with the schema the scripts require (manual_symbols[] with file, byte_start, byte_end, sha256, owner); used whole-file hashes for 9 genuinely-unmodified P0 files
payload.caveat: per-symbol AST-level extraction deferred to a proper sdk-marker-scanner agent — filed in testing/tooling-findings.md

payload.USAGE.md: appended P1 extensions section with examples for WithKeyPrefix, typed JSON, CB bridge, Scan/HScan, Sets, Sorted Sets, new sentinels, perf targets
